items.q: How does sweating help cool you down?
items.correct: Evaporation of sweat absorbs energy (latent heat) from the skin, lowering body temperature.
items.misconception: Sweat is cold and directly cools the skin on contact.
items.random_wrong: Sweating removes excess heat by carrying it out as liquid waste.
items.why: Students don't connect sweating to evaporation; they think the liquid itself is the cooling agent, not the phase change.

items.q: Why is the secondary immune response to a pathogen faster and stronger than the primary?
items.correct: Memory cells generated during the primary response can rapidly proliferate and produce large amounts of specific antibody on re-exposure.
items.misconception: The pathogen becomes weaker after the primary exposure, so the body has an easier job.
items.random_wrong: The body produces a different, more powerful antibody during the secondary response.
items.why: Students attribute the difference to the pathogen rather than to memory cells (B and T memory lymphocytes), missing the role of immunological memory.

items.q: Why does the left ventricle have a thicker muscular wall than the right?
items.correct: It must pump blood at high pressure around the whole body; the right ventricle only pumps to the nearby lungs.
items.misconception: The left ventricle is thicker because it handles oxygenated blood, which is harder to pump.
items.random_wrong: The left ventricle is thicker because it is on the same side as the brain.
items.why: Students focus on blood type (oxygenated vs not) rather than the pressure needed to push blood around the systemic circuit.

items.q: How long is the menstrual cycle on average?
items.why: It's 28 days. “7 days” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: Where is the egg released from?
items.why: Correct answer: Ovary. “Uterus” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: If ovulation is on day 14 and the cycle is 28 days, how many days remain?
items.why: The real figure is 14. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
